results.Query: Add new node with name new_EK_PORT_80 type EK_PORT, to ju1.a4.m3.s2c3. Return a graph.
results.Label: capacity planning, level-1, add
results.LLM code:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    graph_copy = graph_data.copy()

    target_switch_id = None
    for node_id, attrs in graph_copy.nodes(data=True):
        node_name = attrs.get("name")
        node_type = attrs.get("type", [])
        if node_name == "ju1.a4.m3.s2c3" and isinstance(node_type, list) and "EK_PACKET_SWITCH" in node_type:
            target_switch_id = node_id
            break

    if target_switch_id is None:
        graph_json = nx.readwrite.json_graph.node_link_data(graph_copy)
        return_object = {
            "type": "graph",
            "data": graph_json,
            "updated_graph": graph_json
        }
        return return_object

    existing_ids = set(graph_copy.nodes())
    new_node_id = "new_EK_PORT_80"
    if new_node_id in existing_ids:
        suffix = 1
        while f"{new_node_id}_{suffix}" in existing_ids:
            suffix += 1
        new_node_id = f"{new_node_id}_{suffix}"

    graph_copy.add_node(
        new_node_id,
        name="new_EK_PORT_80",
        type=["EK_PORT"]
    )
    graph_copy.add_edge(target_switch_id, new_node_id, type="RK_CONTAINS")

    graph_json = nx.readwrite.json_graph.node_link_data(graph_copy)
    return_object = {
        "type": "graph",
        "data": graph_json,
        "updated_graph": graph_json
    }
    return return_object
